title: First Battle of Ord Biniir

{{Quote|Y-wing pilots bragged of their great victory at the First Battle of Ord Biniir.|Soontir Fel|<ref name="The Making of Baron Fel" />}}
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the '''First Battle of Ord Biniir'''—also known simply as the '''Battle of Ord Biniir'''—took place when a [[Unidentified Rebel strike force|group]] of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing starfighters]] attacked the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]]-controlled [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ord Biniir/Legends|Ord Biniir]]. The Imperial [[233rd Imperial Fighter Group]] defending Ord Biniir attempted to repel the Alliance attackers, but the Rebel Y-wing [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] easily defeated them, capturing the planet for the Alliance. However, a few days later, the second [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighter]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] of the [[181st Imperial Fighter Wing/Legends|181st Imperial Fighter Wing]] recaptured the planet in the [[Second Battle of Ord Biniir]].

==The battle==
{{Quote|The 233rd got mauled by Rebel Y-wings and lost Ord Biniir…|Colonel Evir Derricote|<ref name="The Making of Baron Fel" />}}
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]],<ref name="The Making of Baron Fel" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]<ref name="The Essential Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ord Biniir/Legends|Ord Biniir]] was under Imperial control. The planet itself was protected by the Imperial [[233rd Imperial Fighter Group]],<ref name="The Making of Baron Fel" /> composed of approximately six [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]].<ref name="wing" /> However, in that same [[Standard year/Legends|year]], a [[Unidentified Rebel strike force|large amount]] of Rebel [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|BTL Y-wing starfighters]] attacked Ord Biniir—in what would later be called the First Battle of Ord Biniir,<ref name="The Making of Baron Fel" /> or simply the Battle of Ord Biniir<ref name="TCSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref>—hoping to capture the planet for the Alliance. The 233rd's starfighters tried to fend off the Y-wing assault, but the Rebel Y-wings decimated the Imperial fighter wing, seizing Ord Biniir.<ref name="The Making of Baron Fel" />

==Aftermath==
The Rebels' capture of Ord Biniir helped prove the usefulness of the Alliance's Y-wings in battle,<ref name="The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels">''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]''</ref> and many Y-wing [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] veterans of the battle went on to brag about their exploits at Ord Biniir. However, a few days after the First Battle of Ord Biniir, the Empire dispatched the second [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighter]] squadron of the [[181st Imperial Fighter Wing/Legends|181st Fighter Wing]], under the command of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Soontir Fel/Legends|Soontir Fel]], to retake the planet. During the [[Second Battle of Ord Biniir]], Fel's second squadron defeated the Y-wings stationed on Ord Biniir and retook the planet for the Empire.<ref name="The Making of Baron Fel" />

In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], four years after the battle, Fel was captured by the Rebel Alliance and, during his interrogation, briefly mentioned the First and Second Battles of Ord Biniir.<ref name="The Making of Baron Fel" />

==Behind the scenes==
The First Battle of Ord Biniir was first mentioned in the Y-wing's entry in ''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'', published on [[March 19]], [[1996]]. The events of the battle were later expanded upon in the twenty-fifth issue of the ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series, [[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 25|''The Making of Baron Fel'']], published on [[November 5]], [[1997]].
